intro.title: The Bibliotheca on the Road
intro.body: Photios, future patriarch of Constantinople, was sent as ambassador to the Caliph al-Wathiq in Samarra. He wrote his Bibliotheca — 280 book reviews — to console his brother Tarasios for his absence. The route is reconstructed from the dedicatory letter.

stops.name: Constantinople
stops.note: Departure point. Photios was protasekretis (chief imperial secretary) when chosen for the mission.
stops.imagePrompt: Hand-coloured plate. Walls of Theodosius at dusk, embassy party riding out the Charisius Gate with mules of books.

stops.name: Caesarea (Kayseri)
stops.note: Cappadocian theme capital. Photios likely lodged at the metropolitan's palace.
stops.imagePrompt: Hand-coloured plate. Anatolian highland town below Mount Argaeus, episcopal palace courtyard with mules.

stops.name: Tarsus
stops.note: Frontier exchange-point with the Caliphate. Prisoner exchanges crossed the Lamus river nearby every two to four years.
stops.imagePrompt: Hand-coloured plate. Border river with two parties facing on opposite banks, scribes recording names of exchanged prisoners.

stops.name: Baghdad
stops.note: Abbasid capital, court of al-Wathiq. Photios's brother Tarasios remained in Constantinople, prompting the Bibliotheca letters.
stops.imagePrompt: Hand-coloured plate. Tigris with the Round City behind, Byzantine envoy received in tiled audience hall.

stops.name: Samarra
stops.note: Caliphal seat 836-892. Photios meets al-Wathiq here in his last year.
stops.imagePrompt: Hand-coloured plate. Spiral minaret of the Great Mosque, Caliph's tent-pavilion city stretching to the horizon.
